title: grok-analytics.yaml
description: Official JSON Schema for grok-analytics.yaml — opt-in telemetry configuration with fine-grained event control, PII safety declarations, provider abstraction, and GDPR-friendly retention settings. Compatible with grok-install.yaml@1.0+ and grok@2026.4+.

version.description: Semantic version of this analytics configuration file.

author.description: X handle of the analytics config owner, prefixed with '@'.

compatibility.description: Platform specs this file is tested and compatible with.

analytics.description: Analytics configuration. No data is collected unless enabled is explicitly set to true.

enabled.description: Master switch for all analytics collection. Defaults to false — must be explicitly set to true to activate.

provider.description: Analytics backend that receives event data.

provider_config.description: Provider-specific connection settings. API keys must use secret references, not literal values.

api_key_secret.description: Repository secret key holding the provider API key. Never use a literal key value here.

host.description: Provider API endpoint URL. Required for self-hosted PostHog/Segment instances.

webhook_url_secret.description: Repository secret key for a custom webhook endpoint when provider is 'custom'.

events.description: Opt-in event definitions. Only listed events are tracked; all others are silently dropped.

name.description: Event name in snake_case used as the event identifier in the analytics provider.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of when this event fires and what it measures.

properties.description: List of property names collected alongside this event. Only listed properties are captured.

pii_safe.description: Explicit declaration that no property in this event contains personally identifiable information. Events with pii_safe: false require a security review before enabling.

enabled.description: Set to false to stop collecting this event without removing its definition.

sampling_rate.description: Fraction of events to capture and send. 1.0 = 100%, 0.1 = 10%. Reduce for high-volume repos to manage costs.

anonymize_user_ids.description: Hash all user identifiers (GitHub usernames, X handles) with a one-way function before sending to the provider.

data_retention_days.description: Number of days the analytics provider retains event data. Align with your legal jurisdiction's requirements (GDPR recommends minimum necessary; typical 30–365).

opt_out_roles.description: User roles excluded from all analytics tracking. 'bot' and CI service accounts should always be included.
